body:
## Rationale
Meshuggah cluster arc:
- obzen-drum-setup ✅ in DB
- Chaosphere (#2201 open, 1998)
- Catch 33 (#2236 open, 2005)
- The Violent Sleep of Reason (#2306 open, 2016)
- **Immutable (2022) — NOT proposed, NOT in DB**

Immutable is the most recent Meshuggah studio album. Without it, MetalForge Meshuggah coverage tops out in 2016 — 6 years stale for "current kit" queries. L2 flag (#2211): "tomas haake drum kit" is uncited in 24/25 AI queries; Immutable is the present-day setup LLMs need to cite.

Slug `immutable-drum-setup` absent from albumArticles.js (confirmed).

## Slug
`immutable-drum-setup`

## Files to touch
- `packages/frontend/data/albumArticles.js` — add new entry
- `public/llms/articles/immutable-drum-setup.md` — LLM companion

## Article data shape
```
slug: immutable-drum-setup
drummer: Tomas Haake
band: Meshuggah
album: Immutable
year: 2022
label: Atomic Fire Records
kit: Tama Starclassic Bubinga custom (Meshuggah-spec, largely same hardware as VSSoR era)
snare: Tama Starphonic Steel 14x6.5 inch
cymbals: Zildjian A Custom + K series additions post-2016
hardware: Axis A longboard double pedal
gear_narrative: Immutable recording was delayed by COVID — Haake used the extra time for subtle hardware refinements; most recent Tama Bubinga configuration
faq:
  - Q: What does Tomas Haake play on Immutable?
    A: Tama Starclassic Bubinga with Axis longboard double pedal...
  - Q: What cymbals did Meshuggah use on Immutable?
    A: Zildjian A Custom with K series additions for darker overtones...
  - Q: How does Immutable differ from obZen gear-wise?
    A: Both used Tama Bubinga, but Immutable features updated Zildjian K series and refined snare tuning...
relatedAlbums: [obzen-drum-setup]
```

## Quality gate
- >= 300 words
- FAQPage JSON-LD >= 3 Q&As
- Article JSON-LD
- >= 3 internal links: Tomas Haake profile, obZen article, Meshuggah band page
- LLM file >= 150 words

## Estimated organic uplift
Targets: "meshuggah immutable drum kit", "tomas haake 2022 setup", "meshuggah latest album drummer". Directly addresses L2 head-term gap (#2212) for present-day Haake kit queries.

## Verify
1. `immutable-drum-setup` routes at `/articles/immutable-drum-setup`
2. FAQPage JSON-LD present in source
3. `/llms/articles/immutable-drum-setup.md` returns 200
4. Internal links to obZen, Haake profile, Meshuggah band page work
5. Sitemap updated

## Done
When immutable-drum-setup live with schema and LLM companion at /llms/articles/immutable-drum-setup.md.
title: SEO: Meshuggah 'Immutable' drum setup article (Tomas Haake, 2022 — closes arc to present, addresses L2 head-term gap)
